name: Antigüedad a Cevico Navero — circular desde Baltanás
region_intro: Mountain biking around Baltanás offers routes through the El Cerrato plain, characterized by agricultural fields and vineyards. The landscape features rolling terrain suitable for various skill levels. This region provides opportunities for active adventure combined with cultural exploration of its unique historical features. Best mountain bike trails around Baltanás The most popular mountain bike trail is Baltanás a Hornillos de Cerrato — circular por las Minas de Yeso, a 20.5 miles (32.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 38 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the area around the Gypsum Mines. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Villaviudas - Monte Reinoso Loop, an easy 11.3 miles (18.2 km) path. This route offers views of the El Cerrato plain and Monte Reinoso. Local mountain bikers also love the Senda del Chorro Trail, a 13.5 miles (21.7 km) trail leading through the rural landscape of the El Cerrato plain, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Mountain biking around Baltanás is defined by the El Cerrato plain, agricultural fields, and vineyards, with unique features like the Barrio de las Bodegas. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Baltanás are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 9 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Baltanás's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the overall difficulty level of mountain biking trails in Baltanás?
A: The mountain biking trails around Baltanás cater to a range of skill levels. You'll find a good mix of routes, with 3 easy trails, 8 moderate trails, and 1 difficult trail available. This variety ensures options for both casual riders and those seeking a more challenging experience.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Baltanás?
A: There are 12 mountain bike trails available around Baltanás. These routes explore the El Cerrato plain, offering diverse experiences for riders.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Baltanás?
A: Yes, Baltanás offers trails suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1318340947/villaviudas-monte-reinoso-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaviudas - Monte Reinoso Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 11.3-mile (18.2 km) path that provides scenic views of the El Cerrato plain and Monte Reinoso without significant technical challenges.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Baltanás?
A: The terrain around Baltanás is primarily characterized by the El Cerrato plain, featuring agricultural fields and vineyards. You can expect rolling hills and varied surfaces, from gravel paths to dirt tracks, suitable for mountain biking. Some routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1323726340/baltanas-a-hornillos-de-cerrato-circular-por-las-minas-de-yeso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baltanás a Hornillos de Cerrato — circular por las Minas de Yeso&lt;/a&gt;, even explore areas around old gypsum mines, adding to the diverse landscape.

Q: What unique attractions can I explore near the mountain bike trails in Baltanás?
A: While mountain biking in Baltanás, you can explore several unique attractions. A must-see is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5797861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baltanás Underground Wine Cellars&lt;/a&gt;, known as the 'Barrio de las Bodegas'. This fascinating site features nearly 400 underground wineries carved into a hill, creating a distinctive 'lunar landscape' with their chimneys. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5560975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Antigüedad&lt;/a&gt; settlement or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5797778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torquemada Bridge over the Pisuerga River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks to see while mountain biking in Baltanás?
A: Yes, the region around Baltanás is rich in history. Besides the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5797861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baltanás Underground Wine Cellars&lt;/a&gt;, you can encounter historical churches such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2756980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María, Soto de Cerrato&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5797677" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Miguel Arcángel and Rollo de Vertavillo&lt;/a&gt;, which offer glimpses into the area's past.

Q: Can I find trails with good viewpoints in Baltanás?
A: Many trails in Baltanás offer scenic views across the El Cerrato plain, characterized by its agricultural fields and vineyards. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1318340947/villaviudas-monte-reinoso-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaviudas - Monte Reinoso Loop&lt;/a&gt; provide open vistas of the landscape, including Monte Reinoso, allowing you to appreciate the region's natural beauty.

Q: Is there parking available for mountain bikers in Baltanás?
A: While specific parking areas for mountain bikers are not detailed in the route information, Baltanás is a town with general parking facilities. It is advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most convenient places to park near trailheads or the town center.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Baltanás?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Baltanás is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is milder. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder temperatures, though the region typically experiences dry conditions. These shoulder seasons offer pleasant temperatures for exploring the trails and enjoying the landscape.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Baltanás?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Baltanás are circular, making them convenient for starting and ending at the same point. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1323726340/baltanas-a-hornillos-de-cerrato-circular-por-las-minas-de-yeso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baltanás a Hornillos de Cerrato — circular por las Minas de Yeso&lt;/a&gt; and the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1318340947/villaviudas-monte-reinoso-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaviudas - Monte Reinoso Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails in Baltanás family-friendly?
A: Yes, there are family-friendly mountain bike trails in Baltanás, particularly the easier routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1318340947/villaviudas-monte-reinoso-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaviudas - Monte Reinoso Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is an easy 11.3-mile (18.2 km) path that is suitable for families looking for a less strenuous ride through the scenic El Cerrato plain.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the mountain bike trails in Baltanás?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on public trails in rural areas of Spain, especially if kept under control. However, it's always best to check local regulations or specific signage at trailheads. Ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them to maintain the natural environment for all users.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Baltanás?
A: The mountain biking routes in Baltanás are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the opportunities for cultural exploration alongside active adventure, and the unique landscapes of the El Cerrato plain, including features like the Barrio de las Bodegas.
tips: Here you will find a small town surrounded by agricultural fields. Just outside the city, there is a monument to the pilots of Spain. It is a real retired military aircraft. In the other direction you will find a monument to cyclist Lance Armstrong.

Romanesque-style Catholic temple dating from the 12th century, although it has been reformed several times over the centuries.
It is interesting to observe the main façade with the cover that still presents its Romanesque origins.

Palencia town and capital of the Cerrato region, dating from pre-Roman times, according to some remains that were found. The church of San Millán, the Hermitage of Nuestra Señora de Revilla, the Hermitage of San Gregorio or the Bodegas de Baltanás, among many others, stand out.

waypoints.name: Monument to Spanish Pilots and Lance Armstrong in Antigüedad
Church of Our Lady of Peace, Cevico Navero
